title: Workbook — Matrimony
html:
Student Workbook

For use after class or in a group. Print this page or work in your notebook.

Remembering the Essentials

  1. What does it mean that marriage was instituted «from the beginning»? Explain with reference to Genesis.
  2. What changed in marriage with the coming of original sin, and how did God's pedagogy prepare for its restoration?
  3. What is Jesus' teaching on the indissolubility of marriage? Cite the passage from the Gospel of Matthew.
  4. Why is the wedding at Cana important for understanding marriage as a sacrament?
  5. Name and briefly explain the four pillars of Christian marriage.

For Reflection and Writing

Answer each question honestly. There are no «right» answers to these personal questions.

1. Jesus said: «What God has joined together, let no man separate» (Mt 19:6). Do you know a married couple who has lived faithfully «until death do us part»? What do you admire about them?

Think of your parents, grandparents, aunts and uncles, friends. Long-lasting fidelity is not news, but it is the daily miracle of grace.

 

 

 

2. In Ephesians 5:25, St. Paul says husbands should love «as Christ loved the Church and gave himself up for her.» In what areas of your life are you called to a love that involves sacrifice, self-giving, giving without counting the cost?

Not only in marriage: also in friendship, family, the Christian community. Love that costs nothing is not full love.

 

 

 

3. The family is the «domestic church»: a school of prayer, forgiveness, and virtue. What did you learn about faith at home —or what do you wish you had learned? What would you like to pass on if you form a family one day?

Sometimes we complain about what we didn't have. But we can also ask: what can I sow for the future?

 

 

 

4. Matrimonial consent is freely spoken by the spouses. The formula says: «in good times and in bad, in sickness and in health, to love you and to honor you all the days of my life.» Which part of that promise would you find most difficult to keep? Why?

This is not about being afraid but about being realistic about what love demands. And trusting in the sacramental grace God gives.

 

 

 

5. Louis and Zélie Martin were canonized as a married couple. Their secret was not the absence of problems but living their problems with God. How can you invite God more into your relationships —as a couple, in friendship, in your family— this week?

A concrete decision: pray together, be the first to forgive, silently bless the other before going to sleep.

 

 

 

Practical Activities

A. Read Ephesians 5:21-33 Aloud

This passage is proclaimed at many Catholic weddings. The «profound mystery» Paul speaks of is the union between Christ and the Church, of which marriage is a sign. Read it slowly, twice. The first time just listen. The second time, underline every word that speaks of love, self-giving, and holiness. Which phrase catches your attention most?

 

 

 

B. Pray with Your Partner (or for Families)

If you are married or dating, take five minutes to pray together: an Our Father, a Hail Mary, and each person asks God for a specific grace for the other. If not, offer that prayer for the marriages in your family or community. Intercessory prayer —praying for others— is one of the most powerful acts there is.

 

 

 

C. The 7-Day «Love Dare»

This week, do something concrete each day for the person you love (or for someone in your family, if you are not in a relationship). It might be a word of gratitude, an act of service, refraining from a complaint, listening without interrupting. Each day, note what you did and how you felt. At the end of the week, review what you wrote. What changed in you?

 

 

 

Memory Verse

«Husbands, love your wives, as Christ loved the church and gave himself up for her. This mystery is profound, and I am applying it to Christ and the Church.»

— Ephesians 5:25, 32

Prayer for This Week

Lord Jesus, Bridegroom of the Church,
who raised marriage to the dignity of a sacrament
and sanctified the wedding at Cana with your presence,
bless all the marriages of the world.
Grant them the grace to live unity,
fidelity, indissolubility,
and generous openness to life.
Strengthen spouses in their difficulties,
heal those who are wounded,
and may the light of your love
shine in every Christian home.
Help me to be a witness of your faithful love
wherever you have placed me.
Thank you for Louis and Zélie, for Joseph and Mary,
models of holy spouses.
May their intercession obtain for me
a heart capable of loving without measure.
Amen.

Print Workbook
